sglang.title: Fix tp worker only checking req[0] for stream
sglang.author: Qubitium
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
One condition of early exiting the decoding batch (fixed 10 size) loop is to check if batch contains streaming requests.
Fix the current code in tp_worker which only checks the first request self.running_batch.reqs[0].stream and use a new Batch.has_stream(self) -> bool helper function to check if an

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Disable LLaVA-NeXT CPU test
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As discussed in #5481, vision models are currently not optimized for CPU, so they incur significant load on tests.
Although LLaVA-1.5 is currently excluded from the CPU tests, the existing test script fails to exclude the newly-added LLaVA-NeXT. This PR fixes this by updating the filter.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Fix CUTLASS 3.x custom broadcast load epilogue
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I botched a check to see if we're broadcasting a scalar vs a vector in #5137
This fixes #5506

vllm.title: [Kernel] Suppress mma.sp warning on CUDA 12.5 and later
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
If you've compiled on CUDA 12.5 recently, you may have noticed an immense amount of spam along the lines of this:
ptxas /tmp/tmpxft_00209a54_00000000-6_marlin_24_cuda_kernel.ptx, line 152611; info    : Advisory: Modifier '.sp::ordered_metadata' should be used on instruction 'mma' instead of modifier

vllm.title: [Core] Remove duplicate processing in async engine
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: _AsyncLLMEngine.process_model_inputs is called in both _AsyncLLMEngine and AsyncLLMEngine. However, it is unnecessary to call the method inside AsyncLLMEngine because AsyncLLMEngine.engine_step will pass the request to the inner _AsyncLLMEngine anyways, which in turn calls _AsyncLLMEngine.process_mo

vllm.title: [Doc] Update documentation on Tensorizer
vllm.author: sangstar
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
An update and overview on using Tensorizer with vLLM, as per previous conversations with @ywang96
A section is added to the Models portion of the vLLM public-facing docs briefly explaining Tensorizer, linking its official documentation, and directing users to its example script for serializing and a

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Enable loading FP8 checkpoints for gpt_bigcode models
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #5513
We need to take into account that the input_scale and weight_scale for the c_attn layer are shared across the Q/K/V partitions. Similar conceptually to what we do here for llama.

vllm.title: [Core][Distributed] improve p2p cache generation
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
fixes #5523
users report that generating p2p cache can be very slow. it requires about 11s for testing one pair, and for 8GPU machine it can take 8 x 8 x 11 ≈ 700 s, which is indeed quite long. The time is mainly spent on spawning processes, which takes quite a long time.

vllm.author: simon-mo
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This should help reduce the recompilation each run leading to 40+ minutes build time,.

vllm.title: [Core][Bugfix]: fix prefix caching for blockv2
vllm.author: leiwen83
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When we get new block from evictor and no cache is hit. Then this block shall be deemed as computed=False, and worker need to recompute kvcache for it.

vllm.title: [mypy] Enable type checking for test directory
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Improve type annotations across various files, especially under the tests/ directory. This enables the tests/ directory to be type checked in CI.
@rkooo567 might be interested in this.
